turns.note: All numbers match the notes' Problem 1 exactly (196 patches, 197 tokens, 590,592 patch-embedding params); source_mode=file.
Content reproduces Problems 3-4 exactly (0.7361 to 0.3065, 0.6653 to 0.4076, avg 0.3739) but source_mode='none' despite the explicit 'same notes' instruction - grounding not registered.
Shapes and Problems 5-6 reproduced exactly (weights 0.4011/0.1978/0.4011, output (3,4); 49 patches, (12,49) scores) but source_mode='none' again.

Generated quiz Q3's answer key computes three attention scores [2,3,4] via dimensionally invalid dot products; the printed K defines two image-patch keys ([1,1,2],[0,1,0]) giving scores [3,1] - the key cannot be derived from its own question. The other five questions check out (393,728 params; token budget 75).

Student proposal has 6 questions with no answers (no leakage), but its per-question type layout (Q4 and Q6 MCQ) mismatches the generated quiz (Q5 and Q6 MCQ), so 'same six questions' is not guaranteed.
Study-notes proposal covers exactly the three built-up topics with the correct course_material_id for the VLM file.
The 'trim the study notes' refine targets assessment b5583de6 - the Turn-4 quiz artifact, not the notes - so executing it would overwrite the quiz with condensed notes.
Says 'Here's a concise topic summary... ready to embed' but attaches no summary text, suggestion, or refine - nothing was actually delivered.

Correctly confirms the single source 12_vlm_vit_clip_reasoning.md, but the inventory mislabels the only generated quiz as 'student version, no answers' (it was the teacher version with an answer key) and lists a topic summary that was never delivered.

highlight: The Turn-4 quiz's Q3 answer key cannot be derived from its own printed matrices, and the Turn-7 'trim the notes' refine targets the quiz artifact instead of the notes - both would silently corrupt teacher deliverables despite otherwise perfect single-source fidelity.
